text.title: tree-testing Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the tree-testing methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Test tree MUST be text-only: no icons, no images, no visual design cues. Visual content biases findability away from the actual IA.

rationale.source: Tom Tullis — Tree Testing in Optimal Workshop

rule.rationale: Visual cues let users find by recognition rather than label match; IA quality cannot be isolated.

rule.statement: Tasks MUST be ranked by business / user importance. 5-8 tasks max; mixed easy/hard distribution.
rule.rationale: Too many tasks induce fatigue; too few miss IA hotspots. Ranking ensures critical paths are tested first.

rule.statement: Sample size MUST be ≥30 per condition for reliable success-rate intervals. Comparative tests (A vs B) require ≥30 per arm.
rule.rationale: Below 30, confidence intervals overlap and conclusions are unreliable.

rule.statement: Per-task metrics MUST include: success rate, directness, first-click. Pure success-rate without directness conceals lucky guessing.
rule.rationale: Users sometimes succeed after backtracking; directness exposes IA confusion masked by eventual success.

rule.statement: Tree MUST be ≤4 levels deep. Deeper IA is a flag that the system needs re-decomposition before testing.
rule.rationale: Findability collapses past 4 levels; testing a too-deep tree wastes participant time.
